data.reasoningText:
Now I have the full picture. Let me gather all PackageReference entries:

**Core/Core.csproj:**
- System.Text.Json 9.0.0

**Web/Web.csproj:**
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting 1.15.0
- Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi 8.0.24

**Tests/Tests.csproj:**
- Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.12.0
- xunit 2.9.3
- xunit.runner.visualstudio 2.8.2 (PrivateAssets="all")


Since there are no version conflicts, I'll create a Directory.Packages.props file at the solution root to centralize all package versions, then strip the version attributes from each project file while preserving other attributes like PrivateAssets. Let me first check if this file already exists.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi" Version="8.0.24" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Done! Here's what I did:

**Created `Directory.Packages.props`** at the solution root with all 6 packages and their versions centralized:
- `Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi` 8.0.24
- `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` 17.12.0
- `OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting` 1.15.0
- `System.Text.Json` 9.0.0
- `xunit` 2.9.3
- `xunit.runner.visualstudio` 2.8.2

**Updated all 3 `.csproj` files** — removed `Version` attributes from `PackageReference` entries while preserving other attributes (e.g., `PrivateAssets="all"` on `xunit.runner.visualstudio`).

No version conflicts existed — each package appeared in only one project.
